task: Write a Python function balanced(s) returning True when the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')' and ignoring everything else. A closing parenthesis must never appear before its opening partner: ')(' is not balanced. An empty string is balanced. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function is correct and handles the edge case where a closing parenthesis appears before its opening partner. The code is mostly concise and readable.
trace:
**
I chose to implement a simple iterative solution using a stack data structure. This approach allows me to keep track of the opening parentheses encountered so far, and I can easily check if there's a matching closing parenthesis for each one. The edge case of an empty string is handled implicitly by this implementation, as it will return True since the string contains no unbalanced parentheses.
